## Release — Blackhole v0.6.0

### ✨ What's new in v0.6.0

The orchestrator no longer runs the same fixed pipeline on every issue. A new **router agent**
classifies each issue once and derives the right execution path from that classification — a
typo fix and a genuine architectural decision no longer pay the same cost.

#### Adaptive phase routing (ADR-004)

- **`router` agent** (new) — classifies every issue into a single `route{}` flag set in one pass:
  scope (needs a split?), ambiguity, external-research need, investigation need, design
  significance, risk-tiered plan strategy, security review requirement, task type. Re-validates
  flags at three checkpoints (clarify resolved, research landed, investigation landed) instead of
  trusting evidence that's gone stale.
- **`investigator` agent** (new) — evidence-gathering only, split out from `planner` on purpose
  (deciding vs. discovering is a real responsibility boundary). `research` sub-mode does
  multi-source, cited external-docs lookups; `investigate` runs a ranked-hypothesis root-cause
  loop before anything gets planned.
- **`planner`** gains three tracks beyond Quick/Standard: `skip` (a 4-line rationale record for
  issues needing zero code change — no planner-worker spawn), `design` (alternatives with a
  trade-off matrix, adversarial evaluation via parallel design spawns, refactoring-impact
  analysis, always human-gated — no confidence bypass).
- **`implementer`** gains `execution_mode`: `refactor-strict` (pre-existing test suite must pass
  unmodified, mandatory decision record before the first edit) and `docs-only` (staleness/drift
  check against current code, example verification, TDD mandate suppressed).
- **`reviewer`** gains a security mode (`security_review_required`) — every finding needs a
  concrete attack scenario, a second independent pass tries to disprove each one before it can
  block merge, and a findings artifact must structurally validate before the PR can merge
  (`V-SEC-06/07/08`).

#### Safety and governance

- **All 7 roster agents now pin `model: sonnet`** in their own frontmatter, closing a real
  incident where a spawned subagent silently inherited whatever model the invoking session
  happened to be running, instead of the intended tier.
- A structured fidelity audit (7 parallel agents comparing every routed capability against its
  full source methodology) caught 6 places where a route flag preserved a skill's *label* but not
  its *mechanism* — including a missing security-path safeguard on the one flag where getting it
  wrong autonomously would be an incident, not an annoyance. All corrected before or immediately
  after their first implementation.
- Review caught a genuine safety-critical bug pre-merge: the security-monotonicity check
  (`security_review_required` can only ever be raised by an automated scan, never lowered) was
  computed correctly but never actually wired into the write step.

#### Documentation

- **README rewritten** as a short executive summary with a "Start here" redirect table, instead
  of describing the old fixed pipeline as the whole story.
- **`documentation/architecture/adaptive-routing.md`** (new) — the full reference: agent roster,
  the `route{}` contract and its safety rules, re-route checkpoints, and the complete
  workflow-coverage table (which routed capability maps to which flag, and why).

### Verification

`bun test`: 224/224 pass. `bun run verify`: 19/19 checks pass. Verified from a fresh clone, not
just the working tree.
